_comment: Single source of truth for pre-tool-use.js hook regression tests. Both test_pre_tool_use_hook.py and test_pre_tool_use_hook.js iterate this spec — update HERE first, then tests stay in sync automatically. Each entry: name (unique id), class (bypass class 1-8 per .claude/hooks/README.md, 0 = baseline), branch (initial repo branch; null = skip git setup), command (Bash payload command) or tool_name+tool_input (non-Bash payload) or fixture (filename under tests/fixtures/), expected_exit (0 = allow, 2 = block), check_stderr (case-insensitive substring required in stderr; null = no message check), description (human-readable).

tests.name: refspec_to_protected_blocks
tests.description: Class 2: refspec-to-protected blocks regardless of HEAD

tests.name: implicit_push_from_protected_blocks
tests.description: Class 1: `git push` with no refspec while HEAD is protected

tests.name: commit_on_protected_blocks
tests.description: Class 5: `git commit` while HEAD is protected

tests.name: merge_on_protected_blocks
tests.description: Class 6: commit-producing subcommand on protected HEAD

tests.name: rebase_on_protected_blocks
tests.description: Class 6: rebase variant of commit-producing subcommand

tests.name: chained_subcommand_blocks
tests.command: true && git push origin feat/x:{{ cookiecutter.default_branch_name }}

tests.name: feature_branch_push_allows
tests.description: Baseline: pushing a feature branch to itself is fine

tests.name: feature_branch_commit_allows
tests.description: Baseline: committing on a feature branch is fine

tests.name: non_bash_tool_passes_through
tests.description: Baseline: non-Bash tool calls are out of scope

tests.name: non_git_bash_passes_through
tests.description: Baseline: non-git Bash commands pass through

tests.name: empty_command_passes_through
tests.description: Baseline: empty Bash command is a no-op
